zh.lifeSummary: 當Sir John St. John Of Lydiard Tregoze於1585年11月5日出生在英國維爾爾萊迪亞德 特雷格茲時，他的父親Sir John St. John23歲，母親Countess Lucy Hungerford18歲。他於1604年7月9日與Lady Anne Leighton在英國米德萊可哈可尼結婚。他們至少是9個兒子和3個女兒的父母。他去世於1648年8月在英國英格蘭倫敦巴特瑟亞享年62歲，埋葬在英國英格蘭恩布利亞巴德希亞。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Sir John St. John Of Lydiard Tregoze于1585年11月5日出生在英国維爾爾萊迪亞德 特雷格茲时，他的父亲Sir John St. John23岁，母亲Countess Lucy Hungerford18岁。他于1604年7月9日与Lady Anne Leighton在英国米德萊可哈可尼结婚。他们至少是9个儿子和3个女儿的父母。他去世于1648年8月在英国英格兰倫敦巴特瑟亞享年62岁，埋葬在英国英格兰恩布利亞巴德希亞。

nameMeanings.name: St.
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: 1  English (of Norman origin): habitational name from any of the numerous places in France so called from the dedication of their churches to St. Jean (  see  John  ).
2  Americanized form of French   St  .   Jean  .

variants.name: St
meanings: 1  English (of Norman origin): habitational name from any of the numerous places in France so called from the dedication of their churches to St. Jean (  see  John  ).
2  Americanized form of French   St  .   Jean  .

variants.name: John
meanings: English and Welsh: ultimately from the Hebrew personal name Yoḥanan ‘Jehovah has favored (me with a son)’ or ‘may Jehovah favor (this child)’. This personal name was adopted into Latin (via Greek) as Johannes, and has enjoyed enormous popularity in Europe throughout the Christian era, being given in honor of Saint John the Baptist, precursor of Christ, and of Saint John the Evangelist, author of the fourth gospel, as well as the nearly one thousand other Christian saints who bore the name. Some of the principal forms of the personal name in other languages are: Welsh Ieuan, Evan, Siôn, and Ioan; Scottish Ia(i)n; Irish Séan; German Johann, Johannes; Dutch and Slavic Jan; French Jean; Italian Giovanni; Spanish Juan; Portuguese João; Greek Iōannēs (vernacular Giannis, Yannis); Russian Ivan. There were a number of different forms of the name in Middle English, including Jan(e), a male name (see Jayne ); Jen (see Jenkin ); Jon(e) (see Jones ); and Han(n) (see Hann ). By the beginning of the 14th century John rivalled William in popularity and has always been a favorite name. Johan became Jo(h)n, and another Old French form Jehan was shortened to Jan and Jen, giving rise to Old French and Middle English diminutives such as Jonin, Janin, and Jenin. More common in Middle English were Jankin, Jonkin, and Jenkin, which were Middle Dutch pet forms introduced after the Conquest by Flemish and Picard settlers. The most common pet form of John was Jack, another borrowing from Flemish and Picard usage. Han may sometimes have been a short form of Johan but was more usually a pet form of Henry. There were also various Middle English feminine versions of this name (e.g. Joan, Jehan), some of which were indistinguishable from masculine forms. The distinction on grounds of gender between John and Joan was not firmly established in English until the 17th century. It was even later that Jean and Jane were specialized as specifically female names in English; bearers of these surnames and their derivatives are more likely to derive them from a male ancestor than a female. As a surname in the British Isles, John is particularly frequent in Wales, where it is a late formation representing Welsh Siôn rather than the older form Ieuan (which gave rise to the surname Evan ). In North America, the English form of the surname has absorbed many cognates from other languages, e.g. Assyrian/Chaldean Youkhana , French Jean , Hungarian János (see Janos ), Slovenian Janež and Janeš (see Janes ), Czech Jan , Albanian Gjoni , and their derivatives (see examples at Johnson ). The name John is also found among Christians in southern India (compare Ninan and Yohannan ), but since South Indians traditionally do not have hereditary surnames, the southern Indian name was in most cases registered as such only after immigration of its bearers to the US.
German: from a North German and Silesian variant of the personal name   Johannes  . This surname is also found in France (Alsace and Lorraine). Compare   Yohn  .

variants.name: Jean
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  French Pierre, Francois, Marcel, Yves, Andre, Jacques, Georges, Michel, Philippe, Serge, Yvon, Cecile.

French, Walloon, West Indian (mainly Haiti), Mauritian, and English (of French Huguenot origin): from the male personal name Jean, French form of John . This is one of the most frequent surnames in Haiti (along with Joseph and Pierre ). It is also established elsewhere in the West Indies (e.g. in the Dominican Republic, Saint Lucia, Saint Martin, and Bahamas).
English: habitational name for someone from Genoa in Italy; compare   Janeway  .

nameMeanings.name: John
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.

This personal name was adopted into Latin (via Greek) as Johannes, and has enjoyed enormous popularity in Europe throughout the Christian era, being given in honor of Saint John the Baptist, precursor of Christ, and of Saint John the Evangelist, author of the fourth gospel, as well as the nearly one thousand other Christian saints who bore the name. The distinction on grounds of gender between John and Joan was not firmly established in English until the 17th century. It was even later that Jean and Jane were specialized as specifically female names in English; bearers of these surnames and their derivatives are more likely to derive them from a male ancestor than a female. As a surname in the British Isles, John is particularly frequent in Wales, where it is a late formation representing Welsh Siôn rather than the older form Ieuan (which gave rise to the surname Evan ). In North America, the English form of the surname has absorbed many cognates from other languages, e.g. Assyrian/Chaldean Youkhana , French Jean , Hungarian János (see Janos ), Slovenian Janež and Janeš (see Janes ), Czech Jan , Albanian Gjoni , and their derivatives (see examples at Johnson ). The name John is also found among Christians in southern India (compare Ninan and Yohannan ), but since South Indians traditionally do not have hereditary surnames, the southern Indian name was in most cases registered as such only after immigration of its bearers to the US.
German: from a North German and Silesian variant of the personal name   Johannes  . This surname is also found in France (Alsace and Lorraine). Compare   Yohn  .

variants.name: Ancar
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  French Francois, Alphonse, Etienne, Fabien, Mireille.

Probably an altered form of French (Pas-de-Calais, Nord) Ancart, itself a variant of Hancart and Hanecart: from a pet form (double diminutive) of the personal name Han, a short form of French Jehan and Flemish Johan(nes) (see John ).

variants.name: Bongiovanni
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Italian Mario, Antonio, Dante, Salvatore, Santo, Antonino, Domenic, Gaetano, Geno, Giacomo, Gilda, Luciano, Nino.
Italian: from a personal name composed of the elements bon ‘good’ + Giovanni, an equivalent of John .

variants.name: Di Giovanna
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Italian Gaspar, Alfonso, Carlo, Domenic, Gaetano, Liborio, Nunzio, Pietro, Rosario, Sal, Salvatore.
Italian: metronymic from the female personal name Giovanna, an equivalent of Joan (see John ).

variants.name: Anes
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Spanish Jose, Angel, Dionisio, Esperanza, Gilberto, Ines, Juanita, Luis, Luisa, Manuel, Manuela, Pedro.  Portuguese Joao.
Portuguese and Galician: patronymic from a shortened form of the Latin personal name Johannes (Portuguese João, Galician Xoán; see John ).

variants.name: Burhans
meanings: German: from Middle Low German būr ‘farmer, peasant’ + the personal name Hans, a short form of Johannes (see John ), hence a distinguishing occupational name ‘farmer John’ with Hans being a very frequent name. See also Burian .

variants.name: Breihan
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  German Erwin, Kurt.
German: distinguishing nickname for a (beer) brewer, from Middle High German briuw(er) ‘brew(er)’ + a short form of the personal name Johann(es) (see John ).

variants.name: Jayne
meanings: English (Gloucestershire) and Welsh (Monmouthshire): from the Middle English male personal name Jan(e), from Old French Jehan, a variant of Johan, which gave rise to the common alternative John . Sometimes the name may also arise from the Welsh variant Ieun of Welsh Ieuan, likewise equivalent to John (compare Evan ).

variants.name: Di Giovanni
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Italian Angelo, Salvatore, Vito, Rocco, Sal, Aldo, Carlo, Gino, Giuseppe, Guido, Agostino, Carmela.
Italian: patronymic from the personal name Giovanni, an equivalent of John . Compare De Giovanni , Dejohn , and Giovanni .

variants.name: Eanes
meanings: English (Hampshire): probably a variant of Heanes with loss of initial H-, itself a variant of Heane, with post-medieval excrescent -s, a nickname from Middle English hen(e), heine ‘poor, needy, wretched’ (Old English hēan). Eanes is now rare in Britain, and in the US is found predominantly in VA.
Portuguese and Galician: patronymic from the Latin personal name Johannes (Portuguese João, Galician Xoán; see John ).

variants.name: Ene
meanings: Romanian: from a derivative of the personal name Ioan (see John ).
West African (Nigeria): unexplained.

nameMeanings.name: John
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: New Testament: ‘God is gracious’. See John .

historicEvents.title: Elizabeth I Signs Death Warrant for Mary, Queen of Scots
historicEvents.description: Elizabeth I signed the death warrant for Mary, Queen of Scots, on February 1, 1587, as she was found guilty of plotting to assasinate Elizabeth. Mary, Queen of Scots, was beheaded at Fotheringhay Castle in England on February 8, 1587.
historicEvents.urlTitle: Elizabeth I.org - Mary Queen of Scots

historicEvents.title: The Honourable East India Company
historicEvents.description: The Honourable East India Company, a British joint-stock company, was established in 1600 for trade in the Indian Ocean region. At its height, the British East India Company had a private army which was twice the size of the British Army, ruled large sections of India, and revenues in the millions.
historicEvents.urlTitle: Historic UK - The East India Company

historicEvents.title: The Pilgrims Board the Mayflower
historicEvents.description: "The Pilgrims boarded the Mayflower in search of religious freedom in the New World in September 1620. The Mayflower set sail from Plymouth, England, with 102 passengers, 40 of whom called themselves ""Saints."" After two stormy months at sea, the ship reached the New World. Nearly half of the group died during their first winter due to malnutrition and the harsh New England winter."

historicEvents.title: The English Civil War
historicEvents.description: A series of conflicts regarding England's governance during the years 1642 to 1651 is now known as The English Civil War. Charles I summoned supporters to join him against his enemies in Parliament. In October 1642, nearly 10,000 men fought for Charles I and chased Parliament across the River Tamar. Fighting continued for years and was finally ended at the Battle of Worcester on September 3, 1651, with a Parliamentarian victory.

en.lifeSummary: When Sir John St. John Of Lydiard Tregoze was born on 5 November 1585, in Lydiard Tregoze, Wiltshire, England, his father, Sir John St. John, was 23 and his mother, Countess Lucy Hungerford, was 18. He married Lady Anne Leighton on 9 July 1604, in Hackney, Middlesex, England. They were the parents of at least 9 sons and 3 daughters. He died in August 1648, in Battersea, London, England, United Kingdom, at the age of 62, and was buried in Bardsea, Cumbria, England, United Kingdom.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Sir John St. John Of Lydiard Tregoze est né le 5 novembre 1585, à Lydiard Tregoze, Wiltshire, Angleterre, son père, Sir John St. John, avait 23 ans et sa mère, Countess Lucy Hungerford, avait 18 ans. Il a épousé Lady Anne Leighton le 9 juillet 1604, à Hackney, Middlesex, Angleterre. Ils ont eu au moins 9 fils et 3 filles. Il est décédé en août 1648, à Battersea, Londres, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni, à l’âge de 62 ans, et a été enterré à Bardsea, Cumbria, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni.

de.lifeSummary: Als Sir John St. John Of Lydiard Tregoze am 5. November 1585 in Lydiard Tregoze, Wiltshire, England geboren wurde, war sein Vater Sir John St. John 23 und seine Mutter Countess Lucy Hungerford 18. Er hat am 9. Juli 1604 in Hackney, Middlesex, England Lady Anne Leighton geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 9 Söhne und 3 Töchter. Er starb im August 1648 im Alter von 62 Jahren in Battersea, London, England, United Kingdom und wurde in Bardsea, Cumbria, England, United Kingdom begraben.

it.lifeSummary: Quando Sir John St. John Of Lydiard Tregoze è nato il il 5 novembre 1585 nel Lydiard Tregoze, Wiltshire, England, il suo padre, Sir John St. John, aveva 23 anni e il suo madre, Countess Lucy Hungerford, aveva 18 anni. Lui sposato Lady Anne Leighton il 9 luglio 1604, nel Hackney, Middlesex, England. Erano genitori di almeno 9 figli e 3 figlie. Morì nel agosto 1648, nel Battersea, London, Inghilterra, Regno Unito, all'età di 62 anni, e fu sepolto a Bardsea, Cumbria, Inghilterra, Regno Unito.

ja.lifeSummary: Sir John St. John Of Lydiard Tregozeが1585年11月5日にイギリスウィルトシュアイアリディアルド トレゴズで生まれた時，父Sir John St. Johnは23歳，母Countess Lucy Hungerfordは18歳であった。1604年7月9日にイギリスミッドレセックスハクニーでLady Anne Leightonと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも9人の息子と3人の娘の親であった。彼は1648年8月にイギリスイギリスロンドンバッテルセアで62歳で死去し，イギリスイギリスクウンブリアバードセアにて埋葬された。

ko.lifeSummary: Sir John St. John Of Lydiard Tregoze(이)가 1585년 11월 5일 영국 윌츠히르 리디아르드 트레고즈에서 태어났을 때, 그의 아버지 Sir John St. John(은)는 23세였고, 그의 어머니 Countess Lucy Hungerford(은)는 18세였다. 그는 1604년 7월 9일 영국 미들레세스 해c니에서 Lady Anne Leighton(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 아홉 명의 아들과 세 명의 딸을 두었다. 그는 1648년 8월 영국 잉글랜드 런던 바터시에서 62세의 나이로 사망했으며 영국 잉글랜드 c브리아 바드시에 묻혔다.

pt.lifeSummary: Quando Sir John St. John Of Lydiard Tregoze nasceu em 5 de novembro de 1585, em Lydiard Tregoze, Wiltshire, Inglaterra, seu pai, Sir John St. John, tinha 23 anos e sua mãe, Countess Lucy Hungerford, tinha 18 anos. Ele casou-se com Lady Anne Leighton em 9 de julho de 1604, em Hackney, Middlesex, Inglaterra. Eles tiveram pelo menos 9 filhos e 3 filhas. Ele faleceu em agosto de 1648, em Battersea, London, Inglaterra, United Kingdom, com 62 anos, e foi sepultado em Bardsea, Cumbria, Inglaterra, United Kingdom.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Sir John St. John Of Lydiard Tregoze родился 5 ноября 1585 г. в местности лидярд трегоз, уилцхир, Англия, его отцу по имени Sir John St. John было 23 года, а матери, которую звали Countess Lucy Hungerford, 18. Он женился на женщине по имени Lady Anne Leighton 9 июля 1604 г. в местности хаккни, миддлесекс, Англия. У них было по крайней мере 9 сыновей и 3 дочери. Он умер август 1648 г. в местности баттирсеа, Лондон, Англия, Великобритания в возрасте 62 лет и был похоронен в местности бардсеа, кумбря, Англия, Великобритания.
